title: Houston Restaurant Patio Hedge Barriers for Seating and Traffic Flow
summary: A Houston restaurant used concrete-mounted artificial boxwood hedge barriers to define patio seating, guide traffic, and avoid live-plant upkeep.
problemSolved: Defined the patio edge and reduced people cutting through guest seating.
scope: Artificial hedge barrier line around the patio seating zone
Concrete-mounted sections at key circulation points
material: Custom mounting plates drilled into concrete

title: Houston Storefront Hedge Screening Over Existing Concrete Barriers
summary: A Houston storefront covered existing concrete barriers with UV-stabilized artificial hedge panels to create a cleaner, more welcoming entry.
problemSolved: Improved the storefront presentation while keeping the original concrete barriers in place.
scope: Artificial hedge cladding over existing storefront barriers
Street-facing entry definition with permanent mounted panels
material: Custom mounting plates drilled into the concrete barriers
documentationNotes: UV-stabilized materials were chosen for long-term exterior sun exposure.

title: Austin Residential Privacy Wall on an Existing 16-Foot Screen
summary: An Austin homeowner replaced a failing live privacy screen with 1,800 square feet of artificial greenery for immediate coverage and no irrigation upkeep.
problemSolved: Replaced a high-maintenance live screen with immediate privacy on a 16-foot-tall structure facing a neighboring remodel.
scope: Artificial greenery cladding across an existing wire mesh privacy structure
Full-screen coverage for a 16 ft tall by 130 ft long run
dimensions: Approx. 1,800 sq ft on a 16 ft tall x 130 ft long wire mesh screen
material: Fence screen backing secured to the existing wire mesh structure
installTime: Two-week project with four on-site install days after off-site prep
fireRatingNotes: The original project brief called for a fire-rated panel system.
documentationNotes: UV-resistant materials were selected for long-term exterior sun exposure in Austin.
